name: The Rift Awakening
subtitle: Four strangers, one fractured world
location: The Summoning Grounds to Sacred Ruins of Aethoria

shard.name: Seal Fragment of Origin
shard.description: A pale white crystal that pulses like a heartbeat. The echo of a world that once was whole.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The Sacred Ruins of Aethoria. What was once a place of light and ceremony is now a hollow throne of shadow. And on that throne — something waits.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: It was a warrior once. A name. A life. Valdris took both and left only the shape of a soldier, filled with darkness and given one purpose: guard the Seal Fragment. Forever.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
pre_dialogue.text: I can feel what it lost. There is no malice in it. Only obedience. That makes it more dangerous, not less.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
pre_dialogue.text: ...I have guided many souls onward. I have never seen one forced to stay like this. This is not death. This is something worse.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Nilou
pre_dialogue.text: Can we free it? After we —

pre_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
pre_dialogue.text: We defeat it first. Then we ask that question.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The Void Knight raises its blade. It does not speak. It has forgotten how. The only thing left in it is the order it was given — and the four of them stand between it and that order.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: The Void Knight staggers. The darkness holding it together begins to fracture — hairline cracks of pale light splitting through the shadow armor.

post_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
post_dialogue.text: There. Do you see it? Underneath all that shadow — there is still something in there.

post_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
post_dialogue.text: A remnant. It has been in there the whole time.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: The Void Knight goes still. The blade lowers. And for one brief, impossible moment — something like recognition crosses what remains of its face.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: Then the shadow collapses inward. The knight crumbles. And where it stood, a pale crystal pulses once — like a heartbeat — and goes quiet. The first Seal Fragment.

post_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
post_dialogue.text: One fragment. Four to go. We honour what it was by finishing this.

post_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
post_dialogue.text: I will remember its name. When we find out what it was — I will remember.

scenes.narration: Silence. Then light — violent, white, all-consuming. Then the sound of breathing that is not one's own.
Ayaka lands on cracked stone, sword already drawn by instinct. The ruins around her are ancient and broken. This is not Inazuma.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: ...Where am I? This architecture resembles nothing in Teyvat records.

scenes.narration: A burst of flame ignites nearby as Hu Tao tumbles through a crackling rift, already grinning.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: Ohoho~! Now THIS is the most interesting thing that has happened to me all week, and I was chasing a malevolent spirit this morning!

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: ...You seem remarkably calm for someone who just fell out of thin air.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: Funeral directors must stay composed around the unexpected! Also this is exciting, not scary. Very important distinction.

scenes.narration: Water cascades from a second rift as Nilou steps through, her dancer instincts keeping her upright.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: Oh... the stars here are a different color. Did we cross worlds?

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: The boundary between worlds is thinner than people think. I have always suspected as much.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: Whatever brought us here, we should assess before assuming we are safe.

scenes.narration: He does not fall through the rift. He steps through it. Xiao surveys the ruins with eyes that have seen the end of things before.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: This place reeks of karmic contamination. Something ancient has been wounded here.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: You can sense that? Are you an adeptus?

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: I am the last yaksha. I carry enough karma to recognize when a world is dying. This world is dying.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: A world on the edge of passing, and something called us here rather than letting it go quietly. Rude. I am intrigued.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The ruins are not empty. Shadow creatures lurk among the broken columns, drawn to the light of the summoning.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
pre_dialogue.text: Shadow-touched. They serve the Emperor. End them.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
pre_dialogue.text: I was just starting to feel restless! Let us go!

post_dialogue.speaker: Nilou
post_dialogue.text: We work well together. Considering we just met.

post_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
post_dialogue.text: I am Kamisato Ayaka. I am glad to have met warriors of your caliber.

post_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
post_dialogue.text: Hu Tao, 77th Director of Wangsheng Funeral Parlor! Amateur poet! Very pleased!

post_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
post_dialogue.text: Xiao. We can trade names later. We are being watched.

scenes.narration: At the heart of the ruins stands a figure barely corporeal, wrapped in flickering light. The Oracle of Aethoria.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Oracle: I called you from the furthest reaches of existence. Four who carry the resonance of the broken Seals. Forgive me — I had no right. And yet I had no choice.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: Explain.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Oracle: The Shadow Emperor Valdris has shattered four of the Five Elemental Seals. When the final Seal breaks, every world connected to Aethoria will be consumed — including yours.

scenes.narration: Ayaka tightens her grip. Nilou catches her breath. Hutao tilts her head with professional interest. Xiao closes his eyes.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: If our worlds are at risk, we have no choice but to act. What must be done?

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Oracle: Valdris draws power from four Seal Fragments scattered as anchors across Aethoria. Destroy them, and he will be weak enough to face directly.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: A scavenger hunt for ancient doom-crystals. Much more interesting than the average Tuesday.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: Tell us where to go. We will handle the rest.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Oracle: The first Fragment lies in the Sacred Ruins, guarded by the Void Knight — a warrior Valdris unmade and rebuilt in shadow.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: It never does.

scenes.narration: As they turn to leave, a cold stillness descends over the ruins. The air pressure drops. The shadows around the chamber's edge lengthen — inward, toward the four of them — without any source of light changing.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: Something is watching.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: ...I feel it too. Something very, very old.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: It is not the Oracle.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: No. It is not.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: No voice. No form. Only the absolute, bone-deep certainty that whatever waits at the end of this road already knows their names. Has always known their names. And is in no hurry at all.
